mainEntity.name: Employer wants me back at light duty, but I’m still in pain—what do I do?

acceptedAnswer.text: You must generally attempt the work if it fits your doctor's restrictions. If the work violates those restrictions, document the tasks and contact your doctor and a lawyer immediately.
acceptedAnswer.description: If you are told you 'must' return to light duty in Utah County despite being in pain, you must handle this delicately to avoid losing your benefits. If your doctor has cleared you for 'light duty' and your employer offers a job that fits those exact restrictions, you generally must try to perform it. If you refuse, the insurer can stop your wage benefits. However, if the 'light duty' job actually exceeds your restrictions (e.g., they ask you to lift 20 lbs when the doctor said 10 lbs), you have the right to refuse. You should document every instance where the work exceeds your restrictions and tell your doctor immediately. A lawyer can intervene to ensure your employer isn't using light duty as a way to force you to quit.
